About

← Text Tools

Text Case Converter

Convert any text to uppercase, lowercase, title case, camelCase, snake_case, kebab-case and more in one click.

What cases are supported?

UPPERCASE (every letter capitalized), lowercase (every letter small), Title Case (first letter of each word capitalized), Sentence case (only the first word capitalized), camelCase (used in JavaScript and Java variable names), snake_case (used in Python and database column names) and kebab-case (used in CSS class names and URL slugs).

When would I need a case converter?

Developers use it to convert variable names or API keys between naming conventions — for example, renaming a database column from snake_case to camelCase for a JavaScript API response. Writers use it to quickly fix accidentally typed text. Designers use it to test how headings look in Title Case vs Sentence case. Marketers use it to ensure consistent brand name capitalization across documents.

What is camelCase?

camelCase joins words together with no spaces, capitalizing the first letter of each word except the first (e.g. "myVariableName"). It is named for the humps that the capital letters resemble. It is the standard naming convention for variables and functions in JavaScript, Java, Swift and many other languages. PascalCase is the same but with the first letter also capitalized.

What is snake_case?

snake_case uses underscores to separate words, with all letters lowercase (e.g. "my_variable_name"). It is the standard in Python, Ruby, and SQL database column names. It is easier to read than camelCase for longer identifiers and is preferred by many style guides for constants when written in SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE.

Is my text stored?

No. All conversion happens entirely in your browser. Your text is never sent to any server or stored anywhere.
